key_insight: Compass shows that prostate cancer detection with micro-ultrasound improves when the AI uses multi-view video context instead of single images.
clinical_relevance: This could help AI tools better match expert micro-ultrasound reading and support more accurate prostate cancer assessment during biopsy workflows.
method: Models micro-ultrasound as a stream of 2D frames across a full study
Combines rotational sweep videos with biopsy-acquired frames
Uses angle-conditioned transformer aggregation plus frame-level and study-level risk prediction
evidence_signal: The method was trained and evaluated on a multi-center clinical trial dataset of micro-ultrasound studies and compared against published AI baselines and clinical expert risk scores.
watch_item: Performance depends on consistent video capture and probe-angle metadata, which may complicate real-world deployment and generalization.
paper_title: Compass: Prostate Cancer Detection Needs Multi-View Context
